text: Canonical Wnt pathway consists of Wnt, GSK3beta, and beta-catenin cascade (Clevers & Nusse, 2012; Hatsell, Rowlands, Hiremath, & Cowin, 2003).  GSK3beta recruitment to LRP6 leads to form un-phosphorylated beta-catenin inducing the stabilization and translocation of the beta-catenin (MacDonald, Tamai, & He, 2009).   Stabilized beta-catenin accumulates in cytosol and translocates into the nucleus leading to beta-catenin activation  ADDIN EN.CITE <EndNote><Cite><Author>MacDonald et al., 2009)</DisplayText><record><rec-num app="EN" db-id="z0t2vdzwlt05d9ewr09vwwd85sdd5wtsarsd&q timestamp="1584950464">93</key> name="Journal Article">17</ref-type><contributo B.
